edges.description: Without functional peroxisomes, peroxisomal beta- and alpha-oxidation pathways fail, allowing VLCFA, phytanic acid, and pristanic acid to accumulate in plasma and tissues.
edges.target: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

edges.description: Loss of peroxisomal ether-lipid synthesis depletes plasmalogens, while blocked bile acid side-chain shortening leads to accumulation of C27 bile acid intermediates (DHCA, THCA).
edges.target: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

edges.description: Accumulated lipid species and absent peroxisomal products contribute to neuronal migration defects, demyelination, hepatic dysfunction, renal cysts, and craniofacial abnormalities.
edges.source: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

edges.description: Impaired peroxisomal beta-oxidation is measured clinically as elevated plasma very-long-chain fatty acids.
edges.source: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

edges.description: Impaired peroxisomal alpha-oxidation can elevate plasma phytanic acid.
edges.source: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

edges.description: Plasmalogen deficiency contributes to dysmyelination and neurologic dysfunction; accumulated bile acid intermediates contribute to cholestatic liver disease.
edges.source: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

edges.description: Impaired peroxisomal ether-lipid biosynthesis lowers erythrocyte and tissue plasmalogens.
edges.source: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

edges.description: Blocked peroxisomal bile-acid side-chain shortening leads to accumulation of abnormal C27 bile-acid intermediates.
edges.source: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

edges.description: The severe neonatal ZSD presentation includes hypotonia as an early neuromuscular manifestation of peroxisome biogenesis failure.
Severe neonatal ZSD often causes poor feeding, linking the multisystem neonatal phenotype to nutritional compromise.
Congenital neuronal migration defects in severe ZSD produce neonatal-onset seizures.
Severe ZSD includes distinctive craniofacial dysmorphism as part of the congenital malformation spectrum.
Peroxisomal bile-acid and lipid metabolism failure manifests clinically as liver involvement, including hepatomegaly, in severe and milder ZSD.
Severe ZSD includes renal cysts among the congenital malformations associated with peroxisome biogenesis failure.
Severe ZSD includes bony stippling of the patellae and long bones, captured clinically as chondrodysplasia punctata.
Progressive peroxisome dysfunction causes sensory loss that includes sensorineural hearing impairment.
Progressive peroxisome dysfunction causes retinal dystrophy, represented in the pathograph as retinal degeneration/retinitis pigmentosa.
Progressive peroxisome dysfunction causes neurologic involvement that includes leukodystrophy.
Progressive neurologic involvement in intermediate/milder ZSD includes ataxia.
Progressive neurologic involvement in intermediate/milder ZSD includes polyneuropathy.
The neurologic impact of ZSD commonly includes developmental delay, although intellect can be normal at the mildest end.
Progressive peroxisome dysfunction includes adrenal insufficiency, particularly in milder ZSD presentations.
Progressive peroxisome dysfunction can include renal oxalate stones.
Some individuals with ZSD have reduced bone mineral density.
Dental enamel involvement in secondary teeth is a common ZSD manifestation.

edges.target: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

edges.description: Increased plasma VLCFA directly reports the peroxisomal VLCFA beta-oxidation block.
edges.source: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

edges.description: Elevated phytanic acid reports impaired peroxisomal branched-chain fatty acid catabolism within the same lipid-metabolism failure branch.
edges.source: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

edges.description: Decreased red blood cell plasmalogens report failure of peroxisomal ether-lipid synthesis.
edges.source: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

edges.description: Increased C27 bile acid intermediates report impaired peroxisomal bile acid side-chain shortening.
edges.source: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

nodes.description: Primary adrenal insufficiency is common in ZSD, frequently asymptomatic in milder forms, and associated with specific PEX1 variants.

evidence.reference_title: High prevalence of primary adrenal insufficiency in Zellweger spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: Primary adrenal insufficiency was found in 7/24 patients examined, with 4/7 being asymptomatic.

nodes.description: Patients with documented primary adrenal insufficiency with ZSD receive glucocorticoid (and sometimes mineralocorticoid) replacement.

evidence.reference_title: High prevalence of primary adrenal insufficiency in Zellweger spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: It is important to detect impaired adrenal function because it has treatment implications.

evidence.reference_title: Adrenal Insufficiency in Peroxisomal Disorders: A Single Institution Case Series.
evidence.snippet: Three patients were on daily hydrocortisone replacement, and 1 patient was on stress-dose hydrocortisone only as needed.

nodes.description: Dental enamel dysplasia of the secondary teeth is a common manifestation in ZSD survivors.

evidence.snippet: Some have osteopenia; almost all have ameleogenesis imperfecta in the secondary teeth.

nodes.description: Ataxia occurs in intermediate and milder ZSD as part of progressive neurologic involvement.

evidence.snippet: neurologic involvement (ataxia, polyneuropathy, and leukodystrophy)

evidence.reference_title: Zellweger syndrome: biochemical procedures in diagnosis, prevention and treatment.
evidence.snippet: abnormal C27-bile acids, very long chain fatty acids, dicarboxylic acids and pipecolic acid accumulate in the plasma of the patients.

readouts.interpretation: Increased C27 bile acid intermediates report impaired peroxisomal bile acid side-chain shortening.
readouts.target: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

nodes.description: Craniofacial features including high forehead, large fontanelles, flat occiput, and broad nasal bridge are characteristic of classic Zellweger syndrome.

evidence.snippet: They have distinctive facies

nodes.description: Oral cholic acid supplementation is used as part of symptomatic management for ZSD, where peroxisomal bile acid synthesis is disrupted and C27 bile acid intermediates accumulate.

evidence.snippet: supplementation of fat-soluble vitamins, and cholic acid supplementation

nodes.description: Punctate epiphyseal calcifications (chondrodysplasia punctata) of the patellae and long bones are a characteristic congenital skeletal feature of severe Zellweger syndrome.

evidence.snippet: renal cysts, and bony stippling

nodes.description: Global developmental delay is a typical feature across the Zellweger spectrum, although intellect can be normal in the mildest cases.

evidence.snippet: While hypotonia and developmental delays are typical, intellect can be normal.

nodes.description: Poor feeding is an early neonatal manifestation that often contributes to nutritional compromise and the need for supportive feeding care.

evidence.snippet: Affected newborns are hypotonic and feed poorly.

nodes.description: Hepatomegaly is a specific hepatic manifestation within the severe liver disease seen in Zellweger spectrum disorders.

evidence.snippet: liver disease that can be severe

nodes.description: Progressive white matter disease (leukodystrophy) is a major neurologic feature of the intermediate (NALD) and milder (IRD) ZSD phenotypes.

evidence.snippet: neurologic involvement (ataxia, polyneuropathy, and leukodystrophy)

nodes.description: Combined toxic accumulation and product deficiency produce the characteristic Zellweger phenotype: profound hypotonia, intractable seizures, craniofacial dysmorphism, leukodystrophy, hepatic dysfunction, renal cortical cysts, and progressive sensory loss.

evidence.snippet: Affected newborns are hypotonic and feed poorly.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisome biogenesis disorders: the role of peroxisomes and metabolic dysfunction in developing brain.
evidence.snippet: Peroxisome biogenesis disorders, of which Zellweger syndrome is the most severe, result in severe neurological dysfunction associated with abnormal CNS neuronal migrations due to the lack of functional peroxisomes.

nodes.description: Profound generalized hypotonia is one of the earliest and most consistent presenting features of severe Zellweger spectrum disorders.

evidence.snippet: Affected newborns are hypotonic and feed poorly.

nodes.description: Renal oxalate stones occur in intermediate and milder ZSD and require renal surveillance and supportive management.

evidence.snippet: liver dysfunction, adrenal insufficiency, and renal oxalate stones.

nodes.description: Reduced bone mineral density is reported in a subset of individuals with ZSD.

evidence.snippet: Some have osteopenia; almost all have ameleogenesis imperfecta in the secondary teeth.

meta.association: Autosomal recessive causal pathogenic variant; PEX1 is the most common cause of ZSD. Specific PEX1 variants in exon 13 are associated with primary adrenal insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Adrenal Insufficiency in Peroxisomal Disorders: A Single Institution Case Series.
evidence.snippet: all the 3 patients with PBD and adrenal insufficiency who were on steroid supplementation had the compound heterozygous pathogenic variant in exon 13 of PEX1

meta.association: Autosomal recessive causal pathogenic variant in the ZSD spectrum.

evidence.snippet: DNA testing is possible for all of the disorders, but is more challenging for the ZSS since 12 PEX genes are known to be associated with this spectrum of PBD.

meta.association: Autosomal recessive causal pathogenic variant; PEX6 variants are a recognized cause of Zellweger syndrome, often associated with severe phenotypes when leading to truncation.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic structure and identification of 11 novel mutations of the PEX6 (peroxisome assembly factor-2) gene in patients with peroxisome biogenesis disorders.
evidence.snippet: Most of the mutations led to premature termination or large deletions of the PEX6 protein and resulted in the most severe peroxisome biogenesis disorder phenotype of Zellweger syndrome.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in PEX genes (peroxins) disrupt peroxisome assembly, matrix protein import, or membrane biogenesis. The result is absent or empty "ghost" peroxisomes incapable of carrying out peroxisomal metabolism.

evidence.snippet: Defects in PEX genes impair peroxisome assembly and multiple metabolic pathways confined to this organelle, thus providing the biochemical and molecular bases of the peroxisome biogenesis disorders (PBD).

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisome biogenesis and human peroxisome-deficiency disorders.
evidence.snippet: Peroxisome is a single-membrane-bounded ubiquitous organelle containing a hundred different enzymes that catalyze various metabolic pathways

evidence.reference_title: Zellweger syndrome: biochemical procedures in diagnosis, prevention and treatment.
evidence.snippet: plasma phytanic acid concentrations may be elevated.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated phytanic acid reports impaired peroxisomal branched-chain fatty acid catabolism within the same lipid-metabolism failure branch.
readouts.target: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

nodes.description: Loss of peroxisomal ether-phospholipid synthesis depletes red blood cell and tissue plasmalogens, while blocked bile acid side-chain shortening produces accumulation of dihydroxycholestanoic acid (DHCA) and trihydroxycholestanoic acid (THCA). Plasmalogen deficiency is implicated in CNS myelin instability.
nodes.id: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

evidence.reference_title: IMPAIRMENT OF PEROXISOME BIOGENESIS IN THE SPECTRUM OF ZELLWEGER SYNDROME (CLINICAL CASE).
evidence.snippet: it is worth investigating the level of long-chain fatty acids, plasmalogen of erythrocytes, intermediate metabolites of bile acid synthesis, or carrying out genetic sequencing.

evidence.reference_title: Quantitative analysis of ethanolamine plasmalogen species in red blood cells using liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry for diagnosing peroxisome biogenesis disorders.
evidence.snippet: Markedly reduced plasmalogens are a classic feature of peroxisome biogenesis disorders (PBD) because plasmalogen synthesis requires functional peroxisomes.

readouts.interpretation: Decreased red blood cell plasmalogens report failure of peroxisomal ether-lipid synthesis.
readouts.target: Plasmalogen and Bile Acid Intermediate Imbalance

nodes.description: Polyneuropathy occurs in intermediate and milder ZSD as part of progressive neurologic involvement.

evidence.snippet: neurologic involvement (ataxia, polyneuropathy, and leukodystrophy)

nodes.description: Renal cortical cysts are a near-universal congenital malformation of severe Zellweger syndrome.

evidence.snippet: neuronal migration defects associated with neonatal-onset seizures, renal cysts, and bony stippling

nodes.description: Progressive retinal degeneration with retinitis pigmentosa is a hallmark of the milder NALD and IRD phenotypes.

evidence.snippet: sensory loss (secondary to retinal dystrophy and sensorineural hearing loss)

nodes.description: Neonatal-onset seizures, often intractable, are characteristic of classic Zellweger syndrome.

evidence.snippet: neuronal migration defects associated with neonatal-onset seizures

nodes.description: Progressive sensorineural hearing loss is a frequent feature, particularly in the milder NALD and IRD forms.

evidence.snippet: sensory loss (secondary to retinal dystrophy and sensorineural hearing loss)

nodes.description: There is no curative therapy for Zellweger spectrum disorders. Care is multidisciplinary and supportive, addressing seizures, feeding, hepatic dysfunction, hearing, and vision.

evidence.snippet: The focus is on symptomatic therapy and may include gastrostomy to provide adequate calories, hearing aids, cataract removal, glasses to correct refractive errors, supplementation of fat-soluble vitamins, and cholic acid supplementation

nodes.description: Defective peroxisomal beta-oxidation causes pathologic elevations of saturated very long-chain fatty acids (C26:0) and the branched-chain fatty acids phytanic and pristanic acid in plasma, fibroblasts, and tissues, providing the principal biochemical diagnostic signature.
nodes.id: VLCFA and Branched-Chain Fatty Acid Accumulation

biological_processes: very long-chain fatty acid metabolic process

evidence.reference_title: Unbalanced lipolysis results in lipotoxicity and mitochondrial damage in peroxisome-deficient Pex19 mutants.
evidence.snippet: We generated Pex19 Drosophila mutants, which recapitulate the hallmarks of PBDs, like absence of peroxisomes, reduced viability, neurodegeneration, mitochondrial abnormalities, and accumulation of VLCFAs.

evidence.reference_title: Adrenal Insufficiency in Peroxisomal Disorders: A Single Institution Case Series.
evidence.snippet: PDs are characterized by abnormal elevations of very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFA).

evidence.snippet: PDs are characterized by abnormal elevations of very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFA).
